channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: Torrential rains in east-central Mexico last week have left at least 72 dead and raised concerns about the government's ability to alert people to severe weather.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: News stories show that reporters may have left the Pentagon, but they haven't stopped working
item.description: Dozens of reporters stationed at the Pentagon walked out this week and surrendered their access badges rather than sign on to new rules imposed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth they said would restrict how they do their jobs.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Daily struggles persist in Gaza even as ceasefire offers some respite
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Bassil Naggar can finally sleep without getting jolted awake by the sounds of Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: TOQA EZZIDIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How Trump's approval shifted after the ceasefire in Gaza, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More U.S. adults support President Donald Trump's handling of the conflict in Israel after he brokered a ceasefire deal in Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds — but his approval ratings on domestic issues remain weak.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Trump says it is not 'easy' for US to give Tomahawk missiles to Zelensky, Ukraine
item.description: President Trump during a White House meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said it is not “easy” for the U.S. to give long-range Tomahawk cruise missiles to Ukraine, as Washington needs the powerful munitions in its stockpile also.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Senators look to block Trump from engaging in ‘hostilities’ in Venezuela
item.description: Senators Tim Kaine and Rand Paul lead bipartisan effort to prevent President Donald Trump from launching military strikes in Venezuela without Congress approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump's authorization of covert CIA strikes in Venezuela raises concerns of regime change and potential blowback from Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: US blocks a global fee on shipping emissions as international meeting ends without new regulations
item.description: The U.S. has blocked a global fee on shipping emissions as an international maritime meeting ended Friday without adopting new regulations.
item.dc:creator: Sibi Arasu And Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hannibal Gadhafi, son of late Libyan leader, ordered released in Lebanon if he pays $11 million bail
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A Lebanese judge on Friday ordered the release of the son of Libya’s late leader Moammar Gadhafi on condition that he pay $11 million bail. Hannibal Gadhafi has been imprisoned in Lebanon for a decade without being charged.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky meets with Energy secretary, US companies ahead of visit with Trump
item.description: Ahead of his upcoming meeting with President Trump on Friday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said he met with Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright and various American energy and defense companies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Former Japanese Prime Minister Murayama, known for apology over wartime aggression, dies at 101
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s former Prime Minister Tomiichi Murayama, who was known for his 1995 “Murayama statement” apologizing to Asian victims of his country’s aggression, died Friday. He was 101.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French police arrest 4 in alleged plot targeting exiled Russian activist and Putin critic
item.description: French police have detained four people suspected of plotting an attack on exiled Russian rights activist Vladimir Osechkin.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester And Emma Burrows, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After years of socialist rule, Bolivia’s presidential runoff tests how far and fast it veers right
item.description: After nearly two decades of one-party rule and a worsening currency and fuel crisis, Bolivia is shifting to the right.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre And Carlos Valdez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Teen street musician jailed for leading anti-Putin song performance in St. Petersburg: report
item.description: Diana Loginova faces charges for organizing an unauthorized public gathering and discrediting the Russian military after performing an anti-Putin song.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at the hostages whose remains are still inside Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas freed the last 20 living hostages taken during its Oct. 7, 2023, attack on southern Israel in exchange for the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghanistan’s Taliban government blames Pakistan for twin drone strikes on Kabul
item.description: KABUL, Afghanistan (AP) — Afghanistan’s Taliban government said Thursday that Pakistan had carried out two drone strikes on Kabul the previous day, just before the two neighbors announced a ceasefire following days of fighting that killed dozens in both countries and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: SIDDIQULLAH ALIZAI and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Embattled French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu survives no-confidence votes in Parliament
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu survived two votes of no-confidence Thursday that could have toppled his fragile new government and plunged France deeper into political chaos.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER and JEFFREY SCHAEFFER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Remains of last female Hamas hostage and IDF soldier handed over to Israel
item.description: Israel received two more deceased hostages' bodies, including that of Inbar Hayman, who was considered to be the last female hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A former security guard at the US Embassy in Norway is convicted of spying for Russia and Iran
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A former security guard at the U.S. Embassy in Norway was convicted of espionage Wednesday after a court in Oslo found that he spied for Russia and Iran. The 28-year-old Norwegian man, whose name has not been made public, was sentenced to three years and seven months behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signals new trust in Erdogan, raising concerns over Turkey’s ambitions in Gaza and beyond
item.description: Turkey seeks expanded influence in Gaza through construction contracts and peacekeeping roles as President Recep Tayyip Erdogan rebuilds trust with Washington after the ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian barrage causes blackouts in Ukraine as Zelenskyy seeks Trump's help
item.description: Russia battered Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in its latest heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida doctor detained by Israel during aid mission to Gaza describes experience
item.description: Two Florida residents who were detained by Israel during an aid mission to Gaza are describing their experience in a Wednesday press conference.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Modi told him India would stop buying Russian oil
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday that Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi told him that India would stop buying Russian oil, a potentially significant move to further pressure Moscow over its war in Ukraine. "I was not happy that India was buying oil.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: NATO allies agree to increase weapon deliveries to Ukraine
item.description: Over half of NATO allies are now part of the Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List, a program to buy U.S. weapons for the Eastern European nation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Russia releases JFK files to Rep. Anna Paulina Luna
item.description: The transfer of the 350-page document was confirmed this week by Russia’s ambassador to the U.S. and Luna, who chairs a House panel on government secrets.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: US blasts China's expansion of rare earth export controls
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Top U.S. officials on Wednesday blasted China's major expansion of rare earth export controls as a threat to global supply chains, but said Beijing could still change course and avoid steps by Washington to decouple from the world's second-largest economy. U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Lawder and Andrea Shalal, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s swipe at ‘weak’ leaders highlights the shifting global influence of Western nations
item.description: Amid Trump’s visit to Egypt and Israel to celebrate the release of hostages held by Hamas and to usher in a new era of Mideast peace, some say Western influence is waning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amy Bradley’s family unaware how new leads in case developed: Brother
item.description: Brad Bradley described as "purely specualtive" one of the new leads in the investigation that his sister Amy Bradley potentially has a child.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: American who was on death row in China details horrifying experiences
item.description: Speaking in his first television interview since being released in November 2024 as part of a prisoner swap, Swidan detailed his experience on "Elizabeth Vargas Reports.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: African island nation plunged into political chaos as president flees country, military seizes control
item.description: Madagascar's president fled the country, and the military is taking over, according to an Associated Press report about the African nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas holds public executions, tightens grip on Gaza
item.description: CAIRO/JERUSALEM, Oct 14 (Reuters) - Israel restricted aid into Gaza and kept the enclave's border shut on Tuesday while re-emergent Hamas fighters demonstrated their grip by executing men in the street, darkening the outlook for U.S. President Donald Trump's plan to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Nidal al-Mughrabi and Maayan Lubell, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois community and leaders react as Israel and Hamas exchange hostages and prisoners
item.description: In a critical day for the Middle East, Hamas released the 20 living Israeli hostages it still held and Israel released nearly 2,000 Palestinian prisoners under a breakthrough Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: Israeli, Palestinian families celebrate return of hostages, prisoners
item.description: The last remaining living Israeli hostages were released as well as more than 1,900 detained Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Living hostages and Palestinian prisoners are released as part of ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — All 20 remaining living hostages held in Gaza and hundreds of Palestinian prisoners held by Israel walked free Monday as part of a ceasefire pausing two years of war that decimated the Gaza Strip and killed tens of thousands of Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump to Knesset: Israel has won ‘all that they can’ in military campaign
item.description: President Trump on Monday urged Israeli leaders to turn the page from warfare to peace, telling the Knesset that Israel had won “all that they can by force of arms” and expressing optimism about the region’s future.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: From a hilltop village and across Israel: Tears of joy as the living hostages are returned
item.description: LAVON, Israel (AP) — It was a day of joy and tears across Israel. Families, loved ones and friends watched as Hamas released the last living hostages held captive for over two years in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9
